steps.title: Translate the relationship to an equation
steps.content: Weight equals rate times volume, so $x = 150V$, where $x$ is weight in pounds and $V$ is volume in cubic feet.

steps.title: Apply the volume inequality
steps.content:
The volume is constrained: $14.2 \lt V \lt 18.2$. Multiply all parts by $150$ (positive, so inequality direction is preserved):
$150(14.2) \lt 150V \lt 150(18.2)$, which gives $150(14.2) \lt x \lt 150(18.2)$.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm C
steps.content:
• Choice A — $150 - 18.2 \lt x \lt 150 - 14.2$: Uses subtraction instead of multiplication; weight doesn’t subtract from rate. ✗
  • Choice B — $150 + 14.2 \lt x \lt 150 + 18.2$: Uses addition instead of multiplication; incorrect operation. ✗
  • Choice C — $150(14.2) \lt x \lt 150(18.2)$: Multiplying the bounding volumes by 150 lb/ft³ gives the weight bounds directly. ✓
  • Choice D — $\frac{150}{18.2} \lt x \lt \frac{150}{14.2}$: Division inverts the relationship and also reverses the bounds incorrectly. ✗
That is the correct answer.
